question_text: How does pressure change as you go deeper into the ocean?

options.label: A
options.text: Pressure decreases because water is less dense deep down

options.label: B
options.text: Pressure increases because there is more water above pushing down

options.label: C
options.text: Pressure stays the same at all depths

correct_answer: Pressure increases because there is more water above pushing down
explanation: As you go deeper into the ocean, there is more water above you. The weight of all this water pushes down, creating greater pressure at greater depths. This is why deep-sea creatures are adapted to withstand very high pressures.
